findings.title: Heartbeat can remotely change client log sampling without response authentication
findings.impact: The new HeartbeatRspPb.log_sample_config field is applied by clients on every heartbeat, but HeartbeatRspPb has no timestamp/signature fields and the client-side heartbeat paths do not verify a signed response before calling MaybeApplyLogSampleConfig. A spoofed, tampered, or misrouted heartbeat response on a transport configuration without response integrity can set request/access/diagnostic sampling to zero and suppress client-side request and access logs, weakening auditability during the attack window.
findings.fix: Protect log_sample_config with response integrity before applying it: add and populate signed response metadata for HeartbeatRspPb and verify it on the client, or only apply sampler config from an already authenticated channel/registration response with explicit verification and ignore unauthenticated heartbeat updates.
